item_spec.concrete_change:
Make the quota cascade first-class and SINGLE-SOURCED rather than reinventing it. Verified in source: the cascade mechanism mostly already exists but is (a) duplicated inline across the two scheduling call sites (cli.ts legacy + dispatch.ts canonical) — the exact drift that caused the central regression — and (b) missing the provider-based rung (resolveLimits ignores providerName, destructured as `_providerName` at limits.ts:51). Two existing axes to preserve: the LIMITS axis (mergeDiscoveredLimits provider+cache, then resolveLimits) and the SNAPSHOT/remaining axis (CompositeQuotaSource of QuotaSources). Changes within the four in-scope files:

(1) packages/shared/src/quota/compositeQuotaSource.ts: add an exported factory that centralizes the snapshot-cascade ORDERING so both dispatch paths compose it identically: `export function buildQuotaSource(options?: { halfLifeHours?: number; additionalSources?: QuotaSource[] }): QuotaSource { return new CompositeQuotaSource([...(options?.additionalSources ?? []), new LearnedQuotaSource(options?.halfLifeHours ?? 24)]); }` (import LearnedQuotaSource). additionalSources are tried FIRST (e.g. a future provider-backed remaining-quota source from FINDING-005), with LearnedQuotaSource as the reactive fallback rung. Keep the existing first-non-null, skip-on-throw loop unchanged (it already guarantees graceful degradation). Add a doc comment enumerating the snapshot rung order.

(2) packages/shared/src/quota/limits.ts: activate the provider-based rung in resolveLimits — stop ignoring providerName. Insert a rung AFTER known-model metadata and BEFORE the generic default: when neither explicit config nor a known model applies, derive a conservative provider-typed default keyed on classifyProvider(providerName), returning source 'provider_default' with confidence 'low'. KEEP numeric defaults conservative — do NOT invent speculative large context windows; the provider-based CONCURRENCY behavior already lives in the scheduler's classifyProvider fallback, so this rung is primarily about labeling source/confidence and (optionally) a safe per-type context/output default. Final documented order in resolveLimits: explicit_config (high) -> known_metadata (medium) -> provider_default (low) -> generic default (low). Add a comment listing the rungs.

(3) packages/shared/src/quota/learnedQuotaSource.ts: no behavioral change required (it is a correct reactive rung and readQuotaState failures are already absorbed by CompositeQuotaSource's try/catch). Optionally add a defensive .catch within queryCurrentUsage only if a direct (non-composite) caller exists; otherwise leave as-is.

(4) packages/shared/src/quota/quotaSource.ts: no change (the QuotaSource/QuotaUsageSnapshot contract is sufficient; snapshot.source already carries which rung answered).

CROSS-REFERENCE (drift prevention — the root cause): FINDING-002's canonical wiring and the legacy cli.ts path must BOTH call buildQuotaSource(...) instead of constructing `new CompositeQuotaSource([new LearnedQuotaSource(...)])` inline. That is the single most important outcome of this finding: one ordering, used everywhere. (The call-site edits live in FINDING-002's files.) This is a @audit-tools/shared change: rebuild shared and typecheck both dependents.

tests_to_write.name: resolveLimits cascades in documented order including the provider-based rung
assertions: An explicit per-model quota config returns source 'explicit_config' / confidence 'high'.
A known model with no explicit config returns source 'known_metadata' / confidence 'medium'.
No config and an unknown model with a known providerName returns source 'provider_default' / confidence 'low' (providerName is now used, not ignored).
A hosted vs local providerName yields the conservative provider-typed default without inventing oversized context windows.
A fully unknown provider falls through to the generic default.

tests_to_write.name: buildQuotaSource composes the snapshot cascade in a single shared ordering
assertions: buildQuotaSource() returns a CompositeQuotaSource whose last rung is a LearnedQuotaSource.
An additionalSources entry that returns a snapshot takes precedence over the learned rung (additional sources tried first).
When an additional source throws, it is skipped and the learned rung is consulted (no rejection propagates).
When every rung yields null, queryCurrentUsage resolves to null (graceful first-contact).
